title: Cascading Timeout in Order Pipeline - Missing Database Index
root_cause: A missing index on the `sku` column of the `stock` table in the inventory database caused full table scans when processing inventory check queries. As order volume increased during a flash sale, query latency spiked from 15ms to 2400ms+, triggering cascading timeouts: inventory-service timeouts caused order-service to exhaust retries, which saturated the api-gateway thread pool, ultimately making the gateway unresponsive.
resolution: DBA applied emergency index: CREATE INDEX idx_stock_sku ON stock(sku). Query latency dropped to 25ms within 3 minutes. Circuit breakers recovered automatically. 340 orders were affected and reprocessed after recovery.

timeline.event: First query timeouts in inventory-service (3000ms deadline)
order-service reports failed inventory checks, orders failing
api-gateway circuit breaker opens for order-service
api-gateway thread pool exhausted — gateway unresponsive
DBA applies emergency index on stock.sku column
Query latency normalizes, services begin recovering

lessons_learned: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on all queries in staging before production deployment
Implement circuit breakers on all inter-service calls
Configure downstream timeout < upstream timeout (timeout hierarchy)
Add database query performance testing to CI/CD pipeline
Monitor seq_scan vs idx_scan ratio in pg_stat_user_tables
Implement bulkhead pattern to isolate critical vs non-critical dependencies
